claim: Discovery-memory promotion should require prospective update metadata so scientific hypotheses can be revised or retired when later evidence contradicts the original evidence window.

source_status.caveat: Not peer reviewed; reported benchmark is limited to selected topics and post-window grading design.
Protocol-level memory privacy work; not specific to biomedical validation.
Award page describes infrastructure project, not completed public benchmark results.

privacy_status.allowed_reuse_scope: Reusable as a public research-organization gate and synthetic evaluation fixture only.
privacy_status.consent_boundary: No personal health or veterinary records are included in this packet.

evidence_window.rationale: Captures current frontier work on continuous hypothesis updating, long-horizon memory, privacy-preserving agent memory, biology-agent benchmarks, and veterinary benchmark infrastructure.

update_trigger.minimum_signal: A source revision, benchmark release, failed reproduction, or institution-published dataset that changes claim confidence or boundary conditions.

post_window_validation_signal: At least one post-window source confirms, weakens, falsifies, or narrows the promoted hypothesis or its benchmark criterion.
missingness: No direct reproduction of CKM benchmark results in this repository yet.
No public veterinary benchmark artifact from the Cornell project was found in this run.
No private or clinical data was used to test privacy behavior.
revision_reason: Previous gates captured workflow, memory, privacy, species, and benchmark boundaries but did not isolate prospective evidence-window updating.
falsification_route: Compare curator performance on stale-claim detection using packets with and without prospective update metadata; retire this gate if it does not improve detection or safe claim retirement.
next_executable_action: Run the regression test for the prospective evidence update validator.
